chrome.payment_warning: **Warning:** Your trial ends in less than 5 days and no payment method has been added.

llm.cannot_delete_default: Cannot delete the default provider. Select another provider as the default prior to deleting this one.
llm.delete_warning: All LLM models from provider **{{name}}** will be removed and unavailable for future chats. Chat history will be preserved.
llm.delete_warning_last: Connect another provider to continue using chats.
llm.default_model_description: This model will be used by Onyx by default in your chats.
llm.no_providers_info: Set up an LLM provider to start chatting.
llm.config_disabled_desc: Existing LLM providers can still be used and updated.
llm.add_provider_description: Onyx supports both popular providers and self-hosted models.

form.connect_description: Connect to {{companyName}} and set up your {{productName}} models.
form.display_name_desc: Used to identify this provider in the app.
form.api_key_desc: Paste your API key from {{providerName}} to access your models.
form.api_key_desc_generic: Paste your API key to access your models.
form.api_base_openrouter_desc: Paste your OpenRouter-compatible endpoint URL or use OpenRouter API directly.
form.containerized_host_note: With Onyx running in a container, `host.docker.internal` acts like `localhost` inside the container.
form.models_desc: Select models to make available for this provider.
form.auto_update_desc: Update the available models when new models are released.
form.no_agents_added_desc: This provider will not be used by any agents.
form.fill_required: Please fill in all required fields.

vertex_ai.auth_method_desc: Choose how Onyx should authenticate with Google Vertex AI.
vertex_ai.service_account_desc: Upload a GCP service account key JSON file
vertex_ai.workload_identity_desc: Use the pod's ambient GCP credentials (GKE Workload Identity)
vertex_ai.region_desc: Region where your Google Vertex AI models are hosted. See full list of regions supported at Google Cloud.
vertex_ai.api_key_desc: Attach your API key JSON from Google Cloud to access your models.
vertex_ai.workload_identity_info: Onyx will use the pod's ambient Google Cloud credentials (via google.auth.default). Ensure the Kubernetes ServiceAccount is bound to a GCP Service Account with access to Vertex AI.
vertex_ai.gcp_project_desc: The GCP project where Vertex AI is enabled. Required because ADC cannot reliably infer the target project under service-account impersonation.

chat_preferences.auth_tooltip: Authenticate this MCP server before enabling its tools.
chat_preferences.invalid_days: Enter a whole number of days between 1 and {{max}}.
chat_preferences.header_description: Organization-wide chat settings and defaults. Users can override some of these in their personal settings.
chat_preferences.search_mode_tier_tooltip: Search Mode requires the Business or Enterprise plan.
chat_preferences.search_mode_connectors_tooltip: Set up connectors to use Search Mode
chat_preferences.search_mode_description: UI mode for quick document search across your organization.
chat_preferences.multimodel_description: Allow multiple models to generate responses in parallel in chat.
chat_preferences.deep_research_description: Agentic research system that works across the web and connected sources. Uses significantly more tokens per query.
chat_preferences.auto_scroll_description: Automatically scroll to new content as chat generates response. Users can override this in their personal settings.
chat_preferences.temperature_control_description: Let users adjust the temperature (creativity) of model responses from the model picker in chat.
chat_preferences.team_name_description: This is added to all chat sessions as additional context to provide a richer/customized experience.
chat_preferences.team_context_description: Users can also provide additional individual context in their personal settings.
chat_preferences.team_context_placeholder: Describe your team and how Onyx should behave.
chat_preferences.system_prompt_description: Base prompt for all chats, agents, and projects. Modify with caution: Significant changes may degrade response quality.
chat_preferences.actions_tools_description: Tools and capabilities available for chat to use. This does not apply to agents.
chat_preferences.tool_internal_search_desc: Search through your organization's connected knowledge base and documents.
chat_preferences.tool_image_gen_desc: Generate and manipulate images using AI-powered tools.
chat_preferences.tool_image_gen_disabled_tooltip: Image generation requires a configured model. Set one up under Configuration > Image Generation, or ask an admin.
chat_preferences.tool_web_search_desc: Search the web for real-time information and up-to-date results.
chat_preferences.tool_open_url_desc: Fetch and read content from web URLs.
chat_preferences.tool_coding_agent_desc: Investigate a GitHub repository and answer questions about its code.
chat_preferences.keep_chat_history_description: Specify how long Onyx should retain chats in your organization.
chat_preferences.keep_chat_history_tooltip: Chat history retention is an Enterprise Plan feature.
chat_preferences.query_history_visibility_description: Control how your organization's full chat history appears in the Admin Panel.
chat_preferences.query_history_show_user_desc: All queries are visible to admins and linked to individual users.
chat_preferences.query_history_anonymized_desc: Queries are visible to admins with user identity removed
chat_preferences.file_attachment_limit_description: Files attached in chats and projects must fit within both limits to be accepted. Larger files increase latency, memory usage, and token costs.
chat_preferences.allow_anonymous_description: Allow anyone to start chats without logging in. They do not see any other chats and cannot create agents or update settings.
chat_preferences.always_start_agent_description: This removes the default chat. Users will always start in an agent, and new chats will be created in their last active agent. Set featured agents to help new users get started.
chat_preferences.prompt_modal_description: This base prompt is prepended to all chats, agents, and projects.
chat_preferences.prompt_placeholders_note:
You can use the following placeholders in your prompt:
`{{CURRENT_DATETIME}}` - Current date and day of the week in a human-readable format.
`{{CITATION_GUIDANCE}}` - Instructions for providing citations when facts are retrieved from search tools.
Only included when search tools are used.
`{{REMINDER_TAG_DESCRIPTION}}` - Instructions for how to interpret system reminders in user messages.
chat_preferences.modify_caution_desc: System prompt affects all chats, agents, and projects. Significant changes may degrade response quality.

security.header_description: Runtime-configurable security settings. Unset values fall back to your deployment's environment configuration.
security.sync_expiry: Sync Session Expiry with Identity Provider
security.sync_expiry_description: Log users out when the upstream OAuth/OIDC provider session expires.
security.restrict_email_domains_description: Limit new user registrations to specific email domains.
security.allowed_email_domains_description: New users can only register new accounts with emails in this domain list.
security.password_policy_description: Requirements for all new passwords. Applies to basic auth only.
security.user_dir_visibility_description: Exact name and email lookups work regardless of this setting.
security.user_dir_visible_all_description: Anyone signed in can see the full user list when sharing resources.
security.user_dir_admins_only_description: Only admins can see the full user list.
security.mask_credentials_description: Display format for saved API keys and credentials for admins.
security.credentials_partially_masked_description: Show only the first and last few characters (e.g. abcd...wxyz).
security.credentials_fully_visible_description: Show the full credential value to admins.
security.ssrf_protection_description: Validate outbound requests against private or internal IPs for Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) protection.
security.ssrf_validate_all_description: Most restrictive. All outbound requests refuse to reach private or internal IPs, including web connectors.
security.ssrf_validate_llm_description: Validate all LLM-initiated URL fetches. Admin-configured connectors can still reach private or internal IPs.
security.ssrf_allow_private_description: Like Validate LLM Requests, but admin-configured MCP/OAuth endpoints may also reach private LAN hosts. Loopback (the app host itself) and cloud-metadata stay blocked.
security.ssrf_disabled_description: Use only in trusted networks. Allow all outbound requests — required for connecting to local LLM backends.

skills.title: Skills
skills.header_description: Capability bundles the Craft agent can reach for. Built-in skills ship with Onyx; custom skills are uploaded zip bundles, gated by group grants.
skills.load_failed_desc: Check the console for details and try refreshing the page.

sidebar.enterprise_only: This feature is available on the [Enterprise version of Onyx](/admin/billing) only.
sidebar.business_or_enterprise_only: This feature is available on the [Business or Enterprise version of Onyx](/admin/billing) only.

web_search.desc: Search settings for external search across the internet.
web_search.search_engine_desc: External search engine API used for web search result URLs, snippets, and metadata.
web_search.select_to_enable: Select a search engine to enable web search.
web_search.connect_to_setup: Connect a search engine to set up web search.
web_search.web_crawler_desc: Used to read the full contents of search result pages.
web_search.load_failed: Failed to load web search settings

image_gen.model_desc: Select a model to generate images in chat.
image_gen.connect_prompt: Connect an image generation model to use in chat.
image_gen.disconnect_modal_desc: This will remove the stored credentials for this provider.

common.check_console: Please check the console for more details.
common.name: Name
common.permissions_banner_users_desc: Onyx is transitioning to group-based permissions for more granular access control. Curator and Global Curator roles will be replaced by configurable group permissions. We recommend reviewing current role assignments to ensure a smooth transition.
common.permissions_banner_groups_desc: Onyx is transitioning to group-based permissions, enabling more flexible access control through configurable permissions per group. We recommend reviewing your group structure to prepare for this update.

actions_mcp.desc: Connect MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to add custom actions and tools for your agents.
actions_mcp.empty_list: Connect MCP server to add custom actions.
actions_mcp.modal_add_desc: Connect MCP (Model Context Protocol) server to add custom actions.
actions_mcp.modal_manage_desc: Update your MCP server configuration and manage authentication.
actions_mcp.description_placeholder: More details about the MCP server
actions_mcp.server_url_desc: Only connect to servers you trust. You are responsible for actions taken with this connection and keeping your tools updated.

actions_open_api.desc: Connect OpenAPI servers to add custom actions and tools for your agents.
actions_open_api.empty_list: Add custom actions from OpenAPI schemas.
actions_open_api.modal_add_desc: Add OpenAPI schema to add custom actions.
actions_open_api.modal_edit_desc: Update the OpenAPI schema for this action.
actions_open_api.schema_desc: Specify an OpenAPI schema that defines the APIs you want to make available as part of this action. You can use the placeholders `CHAT_SESSION_ID`, `MESSAGE_ID`, `USER_ID`, and `USER_EMAIL` anywhere in the schema (e.g. server URL, paths, parameter defaults) and they will be replaced with the current request's values at call time. Learn more about [OpenAPI actions]({{docsUrl}}).
actions_open_api.no_actions_desc: Provide OpenAPI schema to preview actions here.
actions_open_api.url_warning: URL found in the schema. Only connect to servers you trust.

scim.desc: Configure SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) to synchronize users from your identity provider.
scim.token_desc: Save this key before continuing. It won't be shown again.
scim.regenerate_token_desc: Your current SCIM token will be revoked and a new token will be generated. You will need to update the token on your identity provider before SCIM provisioning will resume.
scim.sync_desc: Connect your identity provider to import and sync users and groups.
scim.provide_key_desc: Provide the SCIM key to your identity provider to begin syncing users and groups.
scim.page_desc: Sync users and groups via System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) protocol.
scim.load_status_failed: Failed to load SCIM token status.
scim.something_wrong: Something went wrong. Please try again.

custom_llm.modal_desc: Connect models from other LiteLLM-compatible providers.
custom_llm.provider_desc: See full list of supported LLM providers at [LiteLLM](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/providers).
custom_llm.provider_id_placeholder: Provider ID string as shown on LiteLLM
custom_llm.api_key_desc: Paste your API key if your model provider requires authentication.
custom_llm.env_vars_desc: Add extra properties as needed by the model provider. These are passed to LiteLLM's `completion()` call as [environment variables](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/set_keys#environment-variables). See [documentation](https://docs.onyx.app/admins/ai_models/custom_inference_provider) for more instructions.
custom_llm.models_desc: List LLM models you wish to use and their configurations for this provider. See full list of models at LiteLLM.
custom_llm.at_least_one_model: At least one model name is required

discord_bot.desc: Configure a Discord bot to answer questions inside your Discord server.

agents.description: Customize AI behavior and knowledge with agents. Manage agents in your organization.
agents.no_agents_desc: No agents match the current search.
agents.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
agents.editor_actions_desc: Tools and capabilities available for this agent to use.
agents.editor_default_model_desc: This model will be used by Onyx by default in your chats.
agents.editor_name_desc: We'll display this name in the app.
agents.feature_desc: Featured agents appear at the top of the explore agents list and are automatically pinned to the sidebar for new users with access. Use this to highlight recommended agents across your organization.
agents.unfeature_desc: This will remove {{name}} from the featured section on top of the explore agents list. New users will no longer see it pinned to their sidebar, but existing pins are unaffected.
agents.access_unchanged: This does not change who can access this agent.
agents.unlist_desc: Unlisted agents don't appear in the explore agents list but remain accessible via direct link, and to users who have previously used or pinned them.

connector_setup.empty_indexing: It looks like you don't have any connectors setup yet. Visit the [Add Connector](/admin/add-connector) page to get started!
connector_setup.federated_configured: **Federated connector already configured.** Click to edit the existing connector.
connector_setup.slack_credentials_found: **Existing Slack credentials found.** Click to manage your Slack connector.

document_sets.intro: **Document Sets** allow you to group logically connected documents into a single bundle. These can then be used as a filter when performing searches to control the scope of information Onyx searches over.
document_sets.name_placeholder: A name for the document set
document_sets.description_placeholder: Describe what the document set represents
document_sets.connectors_help: All documents indexed by the selected connectors will be part of this document set.
document_sets.no_connectors_selected: No connectors selected. Search and select connectors above.
document_sets.name_required: Please enter a name for the document set
document_sets.connectors_required: Please select at least one connector (regular or federated)
document_sets.all_selected_hint: All available connectors have been selected. Remove connectors below to add different ones.
document_sets.no_private_connectors_available: No private connectors available. Create a private connector first.
document_sets.connectors_available_selected_group: Connectors available to the selected group
document_sets.connectors_available_curate_group: Connectors available to the group you curate
document_sets.connectors_not_available_groups: Connectors not available to the groups you have selected
document_sets.connectors_not_available_group: Connectors not available to the group you curate
document_sets.connectors_curator_hint: Only connectors that are directly assigned to the group you are trying to add the document set to will be available.

files.upload_from_device: Upload a file from your device

users.load_failed: Failed to load users. Please try refreshing the page.
users.email_placeholder: Add an email and press enter
users.invalid_emails_warning: Some email addresses are invalid and will be skipped.
users.add_valid_email_error: Please add at least one valid email address
users.no_users_desc: No users match the current filters.
users.scim_sync_desc: Users are synced from your identity provider.
users.restrict_signup_desc: New users must be invited to join this workspace.
users.cancel_invite_desc: {{email}} will no longer be able to join Onyx with this invite.
users.deactivate_desc: {{email}} will immediately lose access to Onyx. Their sessions and agents will be preserved. Their license seat will be freed. You can reactivate this account later.
users.delete_desc: {{email}} will be permanently removed from Onyx. All of their session history will be deleted. Deletion cannot be undone.

groups.title: Groups
groups.empty_state: Create groups to organize users and manage access.
groups.shared_desc: Share connectors, document sets, agents with members of this group.
groups.no_resources_title: No connectors or document sets added
groups.no_resources_desc: Add connectors or document set to share with this group.
groups.no_agents_desc: Add agents to share with this group.
groups.syncing_tooltip: Document embeddings are being updated due to recent changes to this group.
groups.syncing_wait: This group is currently syncing. Please wait a moment and try again.
groups.not_found_desc: This group doesn't exist or may have been deleted.
groups.delete_section_desc: Members will lose access to any resources shared with this group.
groups.delete_modal_body_post: will lose access to any resources shared with this group, unless they have been granted access directly. Deletion cannot be undone.
groups.token_limits_enterprise: Token rate limits are available on the [Enterprise version of Onyx](/admin/billing) only.
groups.desc_basic: Default group for all users with basic permissions.
groups.desc_admin: Built-in admin group with full access to manage all permissions.
groups.no_private_resources: No private connectors / document sets / agents
groups.token_limit_desc: Limit number of tokens this group can use within a given time period.

code_interpreter.description: Safe and sandboxed Python runtime available to your LLM. See docs for more details.
code_interpreter.title: Code Interpreter
code_interpreter.subtitle: Built-in Python runtime
code_interpreter.disconnect_modal_desc: All running sessions connected to Code Interpreter will stop working. Note that this will not remove any data from your runtime. You can reconnect to this runtime later if needed.

voice.page_description: Configure speech-to-text and text-to-speech providers for voice input and spoken responses.
voice.stt_desc: Select a model to transcribe speech to text in chats.
voice.stt_connect: Connect a speech to text provider to use in chat.
voice.tts_desc: Select a model to speak out chat responses.
voice.tts_connect: Connect a text to speech provider to use in chat.

service_accounts.description: Use service accounts to programmatically access Onyx API.
service_accounts.empty_state: Create service account API keys with user-level access.
service_accounts.role_admin_desc: Unrestricted admin access to all endpoints.
service_accounts.role_user_desc: Standard user-level access to non-admin endpoints.
service_accounts.role_agent_desc: For agents: chat posting and read-only access to other endpoints.
service_accounts.account_role_desc: This controls the service account's general permissions.
service_accounts.api_key_desc: Save this key before continuing. It won't be shown again.
service_accounts.regenerate_key_desc: Your current API key will be revoked and a new key will be generated. You will need to update any applications using this key with the new one.

lite_mode.title: Indexing is unavailable in Lite mode
lite_mode.description: This deployment runs Onyx Lite, which has no vector database — connectors and document indexing are disabled, and nothing is indexed. To connect data sources and index documents, deploy Onyx in **Standard mode**. See the [deployment guide]({{docsUrl}}) to get started.

index_settings.description: Configure how documents are indexed, embedded, and prepared for search and retrieval.
index_settings.reindex_warning_desc: Modifying embedding or retrieval settings requires a full re-index of all documents to take effect, which may take **hours or days** depending on corpus size. [Learn More](https://docs.onyx.app/security/architecture/data_flows)
index_settings.embedding_model_desc: Onyx uses this model to encode documents for search and retrieval.
index_settings.cloud_managed_notice: Embedding model and settings are managed by Onyx Cloud.
index_settings.setup_custom_model: Set up a custom embedding model.
index_settings.retrieval_optimization_desc: Additional indexing features that improve search accuracy by configuring how documents are chunked and contextualized. These can increase embedding cost.
index_settings.multipass_indexing_desc: Index documents as chunks of varying sizes to better identify relevant sources.
index_settings.contextual_retrieval_desc: Add document-level context to every indexed chunk to improve hybrid search relevance. This can increase embedding cost significantly.
index_settings.contextual_retrieval_llm_desc: This model will be used to generate context for chunks.
index_settings.image_processing_desc: Use LLM model to analyze and add descriptions to images during indexing.
index_settings.extract_caption_images_desc: Extract embedded images from uploaded files (PDFs, DOCX, etc.) and summarize them with a vision-capable LLM so image-only documents become searchable and answerable. Requires a vision-capable default LLM.
index_settings.captioning_llm_desc: This model will be used to analyze images during indexing. Only vision-capable models can be selected. Updates apply to documents indexed going forward — existing captions are baked into prior embeddings.
index_settings.max_image_size_desc: Images above this size will be skipped to limit resource usage.
index_settings.could_not_find_model: Could not find the selected model
index_settings.cancel_reindex_desc: Cancelling will revert to the previous embedding model and all re-indexing progress will be lost.
index_settings.enable_extract_caption_images_to_configu: Enable Extract & Caption Images to configure this.

billing.business_pricing_desc:
per seat/month billed annually
or $25 per seat if billed monthly
billing.business_features_prefix: Get more work done with AI for your team.
billing.enterprise_pricing_desc:
Flexible pricing & deployment options
for large organizations
billing.desc: Manage your subscription, usage, and billing settings.

bots.slack_desc: Setup Slack bots that connect to Onyx. Once setup, you will be able to ask questions to Onyx directly from Slack. Additionally, you can:
bots.slack_bullet_1: Setup OnyxBot to automatically answer questions in certain channels.
bots.slack_bullet_2: Choose which document sets OnyxBot should answer from, depending on the channel the question is being asked.
bots.slack_bullet_3: Directly message OnyxBot to search just as you would in the web UI.
bots.slack_follow_guide: Follow the <0>guide</0> found in the Onyx documentation to get started!
bots.table_empty: Please add a New Slack Bot to begin chatting with Onyx!
bots.discord_desc: Connect Onyx to your Discord servers. Users can ask questions directly in Discord channels.
bots.discord_token_configured_desc: Your Discord bot token is configured.
bots.discord_token_change_desc: To change the token, delete the current one and add a new one.
bots.discord_token_setup_desc: Enter your Discord bot token to enable the bot. You can get this from the Discord Developer Portal.
bots.discord_servers_empty_desc: Create a server configuration to get started.
bots.registration_key_desc: This key will only be shown once!
bots.registration_key_instruction: Copy the command and send it from any text channel in your server!
bots.desc: Setup Slack bots that connect to Onyx. Once setup, you will be able to ask questions to Onyx directly from Slack.

file_upload.drag_drop_files: Drag and drop some files here, or click to select files
file_upload.drag_drop_file: Drag and drop a file here, or click to select a file

connector_advanced.title: Advanced Configuration
connector_advanced.prune_freq_desc: Checks all documents against the source to delete those that no longer exist. Note: This process checks every document, so be cautious when increasing frequency. Default is {{hours}} hours ({{days}} days). Decimal hours are supported (e.g., 0.1 hours = 6 minutes). Enter 0 to disable pruning for this connector.
connector_advanced.refresh_freq_desc: This is how frequently we pull new documents from the source (in minutes). If you input 0, we will never pull new documents for this connector.
connector_advanced.start_date_desc: Documents prior to this date will not be pulled in

connector_access.title: Document Access
connector_access.desc: Control who has access to the documents indexed by this connector.
connector_access.private_desc: Only users who have explicitly been given access to this connector (through the User Groups page) can access the documents pulled in by this connector
connector_access.public_desc: Everyone with an account on Onyx can access the documents pulled in by this connector
connector_access.auto_sync_desc: We will automatically sync permissions from the source. A document will be searchable in Onyx if and only if the user performing the search has permission to access the document in the source.
connector_access.auto_sync_disabled: Current credential auth method doesn't support Auto Sync Permissions. Please change the credential auth method to a supported one.

connector_form.connector_name_desc: A descriptive name for the connector.
connector_form.invalid_type: '{{connector}}' is not a valid Connector Type!
connector_form.oauth_failed: OAuth authentication failed. Please try again.

hooks.disconnect_body_2: The hook will remain in your account but will no longer be called. You can reconnect it at any time.
hooks.delete_body_1: Are you sure you want to permanently delete *{{name}}*?
hooks.enterprise_required: This feature requires an Enterprise Plan.
hooks.page_description: Manage webhooks to extend Onyx with custom integrations.
hooks.fail_strategy_soft_desc: If the hook fails, the request will continue normally.
hooks.endpoint_url_desc: The URL that Onyx will call when this hook is triggered.
hooks.api_key_desc: An optional API key to authenticate requests to your endpoint.
hooks.api_key_edit_placeholder: Leave blank to keep current key
hooks.timeout_desc: Maximum wait time for a response. Must be between 1 and {{max}} seconds.
hooks.form_setup_desc: Configure a webhook to integrate with Onyx.
hooks.yup_timeout_invalid: Timeout must be between 1 and {{max}} seconds
hooks.point_document_ingestion_desc: Runs on every document before it enters the indexing pipeline. Allows filtering, rewriting, or dropping documents.
hooks.point_document_ingestion_fail: The document will not be indexed.
hooks.point_document_push_desc: Fires after each document is successfully indexed. Push indexed documents to an external destination such as a wiki or data warehouse. Only fires for public connectors in single-tenant deployments.
hooks.point_query_processing_desc: Runs on every user query before it enters the pipeline. Allows rewriting, filtering, or rejecting queries.
hooks.point_query_processing_fail: The query will be blocked and the user will see an error message.

token_rate_limits.title: Token Rate Limits
token_rate_limits.description: Token rate limits enable you control how many tokens can be spent in a given time period. With token rate limits, you can:
token_rate_limits.list_global: Set a global rate limit to control your team's overall token spend.
token_rate_limits.list_user: Set rate limits for users to ensure that no single user can spend too many tokens.
token_rate_limits.list_group: Set rate limits for user groups to control token spend for your teams.
token_rate_limits.list_on_the_fly: Enable and disable rate limits on the fly.
token_rate_limits.global_desc: Global rate limits apply to all users, user groups, and API keys. When the global rate limit is reached, no more tokens can be spent.
token_rate_limits.user_desc: User rate limits apply to individual users. When a user reaches a limit, they will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens.
token_rate_limits.group_desc: User group rate limits apply to all users in a group. When a group reaches a limit, all users in the group will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens, regardless of their individual limits. If a user is in multiple groups, the most lenient limit will apply.
token_rate_limits.failed_load: Failed to load token rate limits
token_rate_limits.time_window_min: Time Window must be at least 1 hour
token_rate_limits.token_budget_min: Token Budget must be at least 1

usage.feedback_over_time: Thumbs Up / Thumbs Down over time
usage.agent_analytics_desc: Messages and unique users per day for the selected agent
usage.select_agent_to_view: Select an agent to view analytics
usage.no_agent_data: No data found for selected agent in the specified time range
usage.generate_reports_desc: Generate a detailed usage report for a selected date range.
usage.bg_generation_desc: Reports are generated in the background.
usage.report_timeout_msg: The report is taking longer than expected. It will appear below when complete.

theme.title: Custom Theme
theme.desc: Customize how the application appears to users across your organization.
theme.app_name_desc: This name will show across the app and replace "Onyx" in the UI.
theme.tooltip_logo_and_name: Show both your application logo and name.
theme.tooltip_logo_only_disabled: Upload a logo to enable this option.
theme.tooltip_name_only_disabled: Enter an application name to enable this option.
theme.logo_style_desc: Choose what to display at the top of the sidebar. Options become available once you add a logo or application name.
theme.greeting_desc: Add a short message to the home page.
theme.footer_text_desc: Add markdown content for disclaimers or additional information.
theme.help_link_disabled_tooltip: Custom help link is an Enterprise Plan feature.
theme.help_link_desc: Add a custom help link in the user menu in addition to the Onyx documentation.
theme.hide_branding_disabled_tooltip: Hiding Onyx branding is an Enterprise Plan feature.
theme.hide_branding_desc: Remove "powered by Onyx" and other Onyx branding presence in the app.
theme.first_visit_desc: Show a one-time pop-up for new users at their first visit.
theme.require_consent_desc: Require the user to read and agree to the notice before accessing the application.
theme.yup_url_required: URL is required when a label is set

chat.chat_not_found_desc: This chat session doesn't exist or has been deleted.
chat.access_denied_desc: You don't have permission to view this chat session.
chat.error_with_response: There was an error with the response.

input.deep_research_disabled_multimodel: Deep Research is disabled in multi-model mode
input.voice_not_configured: Voice not configured. Set up in admin settings.
input.waiting_files: Waiting for attached file(s) to finish processing
input.placeholder_help: How can I help you today?
input.press_up_to_edit: Press up to edit queued messages

actions.default_desc: This action is not configured yet.
actions.search_desc: Search through connected knowledge to inform the answer.
actions.image_gen_desc: Generate images based on a prompt.
actions.web_search_desc: Search the web for up-to-date information.
actions.coding_agent_desc: Investigate a GitHub repository and answer questions about its code.
actions.configure_msg: Press the settings cog to enable.

share.title: Share this chat
share.description: All existing and future messages in this chat will be shared.
share.private_desc: Only you have access to this chat.
share.organization_desc: Anyone in your organization can view this chat.

profile.title: Profile
profile.full_name_desc: We'll display this name in the app.
profile.work_role_desc: Share your role to better tailor responses.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.color_mode_desc: Select your preferred color mode for the UI.

danger_zone.title: Danger Zone
danger_zone.delete_all_chats_desc: Permanently delete all your chat sessions.
danger_zone.confirm_desc_1: All your chat sessions and history will be permanently deleted. Deletion cannot be undone.
danger_zone.confirm_desc_2: Are you sure you want to delete all chats?
danger_zone.toast_success: All your chat sessions have been deleted.
danger_zone.toast_failed: Failed to delete all chat sessions

chats.title: Chats
chats.default_model_desc: This model will be used by Onyx by default in your chats.
chats.auto_scroll_desc: Automatically scroll to new content as chat generates response.
chats.smooth_streaming_desc: Animate streamed responses character-by-character. Disable to render chunks as they arrive.
chats.collapse_pastes_desc: When pasting text longer than 3 lines or 200 characters, collapse it into a compact tile instead of inserting it inline. Click the tile to view or edit the full text.
chats.default_app_mode_desc: Choose whether new sessions start in Search or Chat mode.
chats.search_ui_disabled_tooltip: Search UI is disabled and can only be enabled by an admin.
chats.personal_prefs_desc: Provide your custom preferences in natural language.
chats.personal_prefs_placeholder: Describe how you want the system to behave and the tone it should use.
chats.ref_memories_desc: Let Onyx reference stored memories in chats.
chats.update_memories_desc: Let Onyx generate and update stored memories.
chats.add_note_placeholder: Add personal note or memory that Onyx should remember.
chats.use_prompt_shortcuts_desc: Enable shortcuts to quickly insert common prompts.
chats.voice_auto_send_desc: Automatically send voice input when you stop speaking.
chats.voice_playback_speed_desc: Adjust the speed of voice playback.

shortcuts.content_placeholder: Provide a concise 1–2 sentence summary of the following:
shortcuts.err_both_required: Both shortcut and expansion are required

memory.modal_desc: Let Onyx reference these stored notes and memories in chats.
memory.no_memories_yet: No memories yet. Click "Add Line" to get started.
memory.type_paste_placeholder: Type or paste in a personal note or memory

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.new_password_min_length: Password must be at least {{count}} characters
accounts.toast_error_occurred: An error occurred while changing the password
accounts.modal_created_desc: Save this token before continuing. It won't be shown again.
accounts.modal_create_desc: All API requests using this token will inherit your access permissions and be attributed to you as an individual.
accounts.will_expire_at: This token will expire at: {{date}} UTC
accounts.permissions_limited_desc: Limit this token to specific capabilities.
accounts.revoke_desc_1: Any application using the token {{name}} ({{display}}) will lose access to Onyx. This action cannot be undone.
accounts.revoke_desc_2: Are you sure you want to revoke this token?
accounts.paid_subscription_required: Access tokens require an active paid subscription.

connectors.title: Connectors
connectors.disconnect_desc_1: Onyx will no longer be able to access or search content from your {{name}} account.
connectors.disconnect_desc_2: You can still continue existing sessions referencing {{name}} content.
connectors.no_connectors_title: No connectors set up for your organization.

token_rate_limits.title: Token Rate Limits
token_rate_limits.description: Token rate limits enable you control how many tokens can be spent in a given time period. With token rate limits, you can:
token_rate_limits.list_global: Set a global rate limit to control your team's overall token spend.
token_rate_limits.list_user: Set rate limits for users to ensure that no single user can spend too many tokens.
token_rate_limits.list_group: Set rate limits for user groups to control token spend for your teams.
token_rate_limits.list_on_the_fly: Enable and disable rate limits on the fly.
token_rate_limits.global_desc: Global rate limits apply to all users, user groups, and API keys. When the global rate limit is reached, no more tokens can be spent.
token_rate_limits.user_desc: User rate limits apply to individual users. When a user reaches a limit, they will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens.
token_rate_limits.group_desc: User group rate limits apply to all users in a group. When a group reaches a limit, all users in the group will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens, regardless of their individual limits. If a user is in multiple groups, the most lenient limit will apply.
token_rate_limits.failed_load: Failed to load token rate limits
token_rate_limits.time_window_min: Time Window must be at least 1 hour
token_rate_limits.token_budget_min: Token Budget must be at least 1

usage.feedback_over_time: Thumbs Up / Thumbs Down over time
usage.agent_analytics_desc: Messages and unique users per day for the selected agent
usage.select_agent_to_view: Select an agent to view analytics
usage.no_agent_data: No data found for selected agent in the specified time range
usage.generate_reports_desc: Generate usage statistics for users in the workspace.
usage.generating_wait: A report is currently being generated. Please wait...
usage.bg_generation_desc: Report generation runs in the background. Check the "Previous Reports" section below to download when ready.
usage.report_timeout_msg: Report generation is taking longer than expected. The report will continue generating in the background. Please check back in a few minutes.

hooks.page_description: Extend Onyx pipelines by registering external API endpoints as callbacks at predefined hook points.
hooks.no_results_desc: Try using a different search term.
hooks.failed_load_specs: Failed to load hook specifications. Please refresh the page.
hooks.failed_load_hooks: Failed to load hooks. Please refresh the page.
hooks.enterprise_required: Hook Extensions require an Enterprise license.
hooks.not_enabled: Hook Extensions are not enabled for this deployment.
hooks.disconnect_body_1: Onyx will stop calling this endpoint for hook ***{{name}}***. In-flight requests will continue to run. The external endpoint may still retain data previously sent to it. You can reconnect this hook later if needed.
hooks.disconnect_body_2: You can also delete this hook. Deletion cannot be undone.
hooks.delete_body_1: Hook ***{{name}}*** will be permanently removed from this hook point. The external endpoint may still retain data previously sent to it.
hooks.form_setup_desc: Connect an external API endpoint to extend the hook point.
hooks.display_name_placeholder: Name your extension at this hook point
hooks.fail_strategy_soft_desc: If the endpoint returns an error, Onyx logs it and continues the pipeline as normal, ignoring the hook result.
hooks.timeout_desc: Maximum time Onyx will wait for the endpoint to respond before applying the fail strategy. Must be greater than 0 and at most {{max}} seconds.
hooks.endpoint_url_desc: Only connect to servers you trust. You are responsible for actions taken and data shared with this connection.
hooks.api_key_desc: Onyx will use this key to authenticate with your API endpoint.
hooks.api_key_edit_placeholder: Leave blank to keep current key
hooks.yup_timeout_invalid: Must be greater than 0 and at most {{max}} seconds.
hooks.error_timeout: Connection timed out. Try increasing the timeout.
hooks.logs_no_errors: No errors in the past 30 days.

theme.title: Custom Theme
theme.desc: Customize how the application appears to users across your organization.
theme.app_name_desc: This name will show across the app and replace "Onyx" in the UI.
theme.tooltip_logo_and_name: Show both your application logo and name.
theme.tooltip_logo_only_disabled: Upload a logo to enable this option.
theme.tooltip_name_only_disabled: Enter an application name to enable this option.
theme.logo_style_desc: Choose what to display at the top of the sidebar. Options become available once you add a logo or application name.
theme.greeting_desc: Add a short message to the home page.
theme.footer_text_desc: Add markdown content for disclaimers or additional information.
theme.help_link_disabled_tooltip: Custom help link is an Enterprise Plan feature.
theme.help_link_desc: Add a custom help link in the user menu in addition to the Onyx documentation.
theme.hide_branding_disabled_tooltip: Hiding Onyx branding is an Enterprise Plan feature.
theme.hide_branding_desc: Remove "powered by Onyx" and other Onyx branding presence in the app.
theme.first_visit_desc: Show a one-time pop-up for new users at their first visit.
theme.require_consent_desc: Require the user to read and agree to the notice before accessing the application.
theme.yup_url_required: URL is required when a label is set

projects.create_desc: Use projects to organize your files and chats in one place, and add custom instructions for ongoing work.
projects.instructions_desc_empty: Add instructions to tailor the response in this project.
projects.files_desc: Chats in this project can access these files.
projects.dropzone_desc: Add documents, texts, or images to use in the project. Drag & drop supported.
projects.dropzone_active: Drop files here to add to this project
projects.set_instructions_desc: Specify the behaviors or tone for the chat sessions in this project.
projects.instructions_placeholder: My goal with is to... be sure to... in your responses.
projects.delete_project_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this project? This action cannot be undone.
projects.project_files_desc: Sessions in this project can access the files here.
projects.context_limit_warning: This project exceeds the model's context limits. Sessions will automatically search for relevant files first before generating response.
projects.delete_chat_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this chat session? This action cannot be undone.

sidebar.recents_empty: Try sending a message! Your chat history will appear here.

onboarding.complete_title: You're all set, review the optional settings or click Finish Setup
onboarding.toast_save_name_failed: Failed to save name. Please try again.

final.web_search_desc: Enable Onyx to search the internet for information.
final.image_gen_desc: Set up models to create images in your chats.
final.invite_team_desc: Manage users and permissions for your team

llm.connect_desc: Onyx supports both self-hosted models and popular providers.

token_rate_limits.title: Token Rate Limits
token_rate_limits.description: Token rate limits enable you control how many tokens can be spent in a given time period. With token rate limits, you can:
token_rate_limits.list_global: Set a global rate limit to control your team's overall token spend.
token_rate_limits.list_user: Set rate limits for users to ensure that no single user can spend too many tokens.
token_rate_limits.list_group: Set rate limits for user groups to control token spend for your teams.
token_rate_limits.list_on_the_fly: Enable and disable rate limits on the fly.
token_rate_limits.global_desc: Global rate limits apply to all users, user groups, and API keys. When the global rate limit is reached, no more tokens can be spent.
token_rate_limits.user_desc: User rate limits apply to individual users. When a user reaches a limit, they will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens.
token_rate_limits.group_desc: User group rate limits apply to all users in a group. When a group reaches a limit, all users in the group will be temporarily blocked from spending tokens, regardless of their individual limits. If a user is in multiple groups, the most lenient limit will apply.
token_rate_limits.failed_load: Failed to load token rate limits
token_rate_limits.time_window_min: Time Window must be at least 1 hour
token_rate_limits.token_budget_min: Token Budget must be at least 1

health.backend_unavailable_desc: If this is your initial setup or you just updated your Onyx deployment, this is likely because the backend is still starting up. Give it a minute or two, and then refresh the page. If that does not work, make sure the backend is setup and/or contact an administrator.

error_page.title: We encountered an issue
error_page.onyx_settings_error: It seems there was a problem loading your Onyx settings. This could be due to a configuration issue or incomplete setup.
error_page.admin_documentation: If you're an admin, please review our <docLink>documentation</docLink> for proper configuration steps. If you're a user, please contact your admin for assistance.
error_page.discord_support: Need help? Join our <discordLink>Discord community</discordLink> for support.
